title: Erigeron oligocephalus
intro: Erigeron oligocephalus, also known as the few-headed fleabane, is a plant species in the family Asteraceae. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, with potential health benefits including anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial effects. The bioactive constituents of Erigeron oligocephalus include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Erigeron oligocephalus is often used to make teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat various ailments, including respiratory issues and skin conditions.
taxonomy: Erigeron oligocephalus is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae, commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. It belongs to the genus Erigeron, which includes a wide range of daisy-like plants. The exact classification of Erigeron oligocephalus can be further broken down into the following: kingdom Plantae, clade Angiosperms, clade Eudicots, clade Asterids, order Asterales, family Asteraceae, genus Erigeron, and species E. oligocephalus. Erigeron oligocephalus is a perennial herb that is native to the southwestern United States. The classification of this plant reflects its botanical relationships and evolutionary history.
